basic_details.job_title: ONGC Graduate Trainee Recruitment 2026 – Notification Out for 52 Geologists, Engineers Posts, Apply Online
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Computer Based Test (CBT), Interview, Medical Fitness
basic_details.short_information: The Oil and Natural Gas Corporation (ONGC) has released Advt. No. 2/2026 (R&P) inviting online applications for the recruitment of Geologists and Engineers at E1 Level through a Computer Based Test (CBT). There are 52 vacancies across five streams: Petroleum Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Instrumentation Engineering, and Geology. Online registration starts on 17 July 2026 and closes on 31 July 2026. This article covers vacancy details, eligibility, age limit, salary, application fee, selection process, and the complete application procedure.

application_fee.details: *The registration fee excludes convenience fee/bank commission plus GST. Registration fee is non-refundable and must be deposited online. ONGC departmental candidates must also pay the applicable fee, which is reimbursable to them.

age_limit.details: Category Max. Age (Streams 1–4: Engineering) Max. Age (Stream 5: Geology) Unreserved/EWS 26 Years 27 Years OBC (NCL) 29 Years 30 Years SC/ST 31 Years 32 Years PwBD – General/EWS 36 Years 37 Years PwBD – OBC (NCL) 39 Years 40 Years PwBD – SC/ST 41 Years 42 Years Ex-Servicemen – General/EWS 31 Years 32 Years Ex-Servicemen – OBC (NCL) 31 Years 32 Years Ex-Servicemen – SC/ST 31 Years 32 Years Departmental Candidates Relaxation as per respective category, plus further relaxation equal to service rendered in ONGC | Ex-servicemen who are also ONGC departmental candidates get whichever age relaxation (Ex-servicemen or Departmental) is higher.

qualification.details: Qualifications must be approved/recognized by relevant statutory bodies (e.g. AIU, UGC, AICTE); Distance Learning/part-time qualifications are accepted only if similarly recognized. Qualifications listed are "sacrosanct" - no equivalent qualification is accepted (for example, AMIE is not accepted where a Graduate Degree in Engineering is required). CGPA/OGPA/CPI/DGPA or letter grades must be converted to a percentage as per the university/institute's own conversion norms; a conversion certificate must be submitted at document verification. Only candidates who have already acquired the qualification on or before the last date of application will be considered. BE/B.Tech in Chemical Sciences and Technology will not be accepted for the Chemical Engineering stream. | Ex-servicemen who are also ONGC departmental candidates get whichever age relaxation (Ex-servicemen or Departmental) is higher.

salary_details.details: ONGC states the overall package is approximately ₹26 lacs CTC.

selection_process: Computer Based Test (CBT) Interview Medical Fitness (as per ONGC Medical Examination of Employees Rules)

Only candidates who qualify all three stages will be issued an Offer of Appointment. CBT scores are used to shortlist candidates for interview in a 1:5 ratio per category (all candidates meeting the cut-off are shortlisted even if this exceeds the ratio). Shortlisted candidates may also be subjected to a Group Discussion
which is not of qualifying nature.

CBT: General/EWS/OBC candidates need a minimum of 45%; SC/ST/PwBD candidates need a minimum of 40%. Interview: General/EWS/OBC candidates need a minimum of 60% (9 out of 15 marks); SC/ST/PwBD candidates need a minimum of 40% (6 out of 15 marks).

exam_pattern.details: The CBT is Multiple-Choice Question based, of 2 hours duration. There is no negative marking. PwBD candidates get compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour; PwBD candidates with Blindness or Locomotor disability who need a scribe may bring one, subject to a certificate from a designated government medical authority. If the exam is held across multiple sessions, scores are normalized to adjust for difficulty-level differences. | The CBT will be conducted in 19 cities: Delhi (including NCR), Kolkata, Mumbai/Thane/Navi Mumbai/MMR, Chennai, Ahmedabad/Gandhinagar, Hyderabad, Guwahati, Jaipur, Udaipur, Mohali, Nagpur, Bhopal, Bengaluru, Ernakulum, Raipur, Surat, Lucknow, Visakhapatnam, and Bhubaneswar. | Candidates must give a test-centre preference at the time of application; changes are not allowed later, though ONGC reserves the right to change, add, or reduce test cities/centres at its discretion.

how_to_apply: Visit www.ongcindia.com and register online between 17/07/2026 and 31/07/2026. No other mode of application is accepted.
Candidates may apply for only one Academic/Engineering Stream, based on their qualification.
Keep the required documents (listed above) ready before starting.
Pay the applicable registration fee online (where applicable).
Submit the application; no changes are allowed once submitted.
If multiple applications are submitted, only the latest one is considered final; older ones are rejected.

All correspondence will be through the registered email/mobile only. No physical admit card or interview call letter will be sent by post - candidates must download these from the ONGC website themselves.

rows.details: Computer Based Test (CBT), Interview, Medical Fitness

rows.activity: Last date and time of online registration

content_sections.heading: ONGC E1 Level Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: The 52 vacancies are distributed across five Academic/Engineering streams as follows: Candidates with a Petroleum Engineering qualification will be considered for all 9 vacancies in that stream. Candidates with an Applied Petroleum Engineering qualification will be considered for only 7 of those 9 vacancies (UR-3, OBC-2, SC-1, EWS-1).

content_sections.heading: ONGC Recruitment 2026: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Qualifications must be approved/recognized by relevant statutory bodies (e.g. AIU, UGC, AICTE); Distance Learning/part-time qualifications are accepted only if similarly recognized. Qualifications listed are "sacrosanct" - no equivalent qualification is accepted (for example, AMIE is not accepted where a Graduate Degree in Engineering is required). CGPA/OGPA/CPI/DGPA or letter grades must be converted to a percentage as per the university/institute's own conversion norms; a conversion certificate must be submitted at document verification. Only candidates who have already acquired the qualification on or before the last date of application will be considered. BE/B.Tech in Chemical Sciences and Technology will not be accepted for the Chemical Engineering stream. Ex-servicemen who are also ONGC departmental candidates get whichever age relaxation (Ex-servicemen or Departmental) is higher.

rows.essential_qualification: Graduate Degree in Petroleum Engineering (min. 60% marks) OR Graduate Degree in Applied Petroleum Engineering (min. 60% marks)
Graduate Degree in Mechanical Engineering with minimum 60% marks
Graduate Degree in Chemical Engineering with minimum 60% marks
Graduate Degree in Instrumentation Engineering with minimum 60% marks
Post Graduate Degree in Geology (min. 60%) OR M.Sc./M.Tech in Petroleum Geoscience (min. 60%) OR M.Sc./M.Tech in Petroleum Geology (min. 60%) OR M.Tech in Geological Technology (min. 60%)

tables.caption: ONGC Age Limit 2026 (as on last date of application)

rows.max_age_streams_1_4_engineering: Relaxation as per respective category, plus further relaxation equal to service rendered in ONGC

content_sections.heading: ONGC E1 Level Salary and Pay Scale 2026
content_sections.text: ONGC states the overall package is approximately ₹26 lacs CTC.

rows.allowances: Annual increment @ 3%; allowances @ 35% of Basic Pay under Cafeteria Approach; Dearness Allowance; HRA/Company Accommodation; Contributory Provident Fund; Conveyance Maintenance; Leave Encashment; Performance Related Pay (PRP); Medical Facility for self and dependents; Gratuity; Post Retirement Benefit Scheme; Composite Social Security Scheme, etc., as per Company rules.

content_sections.heading: ONGC Recruitment 2026: Application Fee
content_sections.text: *The registration fee excludes convenience fee/bank commission plus GST. Registration fee is non-refundable and must be deposited online. ONGC departmental candidates must also pay the applicable fee, which is reimbursable to them.

content_sections.heading: ONGC Recruitment 2026: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection consists of three stages: Computer Based Test (CBT) Interview Medical Fitness (as per ONGC Medical Examination of Employees Rules) Only candidates who qualify all three stages will be issued an Offer of Appointment. CBT scores are used to shortlist candidates for interview in a 1:5 ratio per category (all candidates meeting the cut-off are shortlisted even if this exceeds the ratio). Shortlisted candidates may also be subjected to a Group Discussion, which is not of qualifying nature. CBT: General/EWS/OBC candidates need a minimum of 45%; SC/ST/PwBD candidates need a minimum of 40%. Interview: General/EWS/OBC candidates need a minimum of 60% (9 out of 15 marks); SC/ST/PwBD candidates need a minimum of 40% (6 out of 15 marks).

content_sections.heading: ONGC CBT Exam Pattern 2026
content_sections.text: The CBT is Multiple-Choice Question based, of 2 hours duration. There is no negative marking. PwBD candidates get compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour; PwBD candidates with Blindness or Locomotor disability who need a scribe may bring one, subject to a certificate from a designated government medical authority. If the exam is held across multiple sessions, scores are normalized to adjust for difficulty-level differences. The CBT will be conducted in 19 cities: Delhi (including NCR), Kolkata, Mumbai/Thane/Navi Mumbai/MMR, Chennai, Ahmedabad/Gandhinagar, Hyderabad, Guwahati, Jaipur, Udaipur, Mohali, Nagpur, Bhopal, Bengaluru, Ernakulum, Raipur, Surat, Lucknow, Visakhapatnam, and Bhubaneswar. Candidates must give a test-centre preference at the time of application; changes are not allowed later, though ONGC reserves the right to change, add, or reduce test cities/centres at its discretion.

content_sections.heading: ONGC Recruitment 2026: Documents Required
content_sections.text: Candidates should keep the following ready before starting the online application: Mobile number and email ID (for OTP) Scanned passport-size colour photograph with white background (JPG/JPEG, 20kb–50kb) Scanned signature (JPG/JPEG, 10kb–20kb) Class 10th Certificate (for date of birth verification) Degree Certificate of the essential qualification Consolidated marksheet/transcript for all semesters or years Photo identity proof (Aadhaar Card, Passport, Driving Licence, Voter ID Card) PAN Card All documents must be uploaded in PDF format, not exceeding 500 kb per file.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for ONGC Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit www.ongcindia.com and register online between 17/07/2026 and 31/07/2026. No other mode of application is accepted. Candidates may apply for only one Academic/Engineering Stream, based on their qualification. Keep the required documents (listed above) ready before starting. Pay the applicable registration fee online (where applicable). Submit the application; no changes are allowed once submitted. If multiple applications are submitted, only the latest one is considered final; older ones are rejected. All correspondence will be through the registered email/mobile only. No physical admit card or interview call letter will be sent by post - candidates must download these from the ONGC website themselves.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in ONGC Recruitment 2026 (Advt. No. 2/2026)?
faq.answer: There are 52 vacancies for Geologists and Engineers at E1 Level across Petroleum Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Instrumentation Engineering, and Geology streams.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for ONGC E1 Level Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online registration window closes on 31 July 2026 at 23:59 Hours. Registration opened on 17 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for ONGC E1 Level posts?
faq.answer: The E1 level Basic Pay Scale is ₹60,000–1,80,000/-, plus allowances, with an approximate total compensation of ₹26 lacs CTC.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for ONGC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: For Unreserved/EWS candidates, the maximum age is 26 years for the Engineering streams and 27 years for Geology, as on the last date of application, with relaxation for OBC (NCL), SC/ST, PwBD, and Ex-servicemen categories.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for ONGC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: GEN/OBC/EWS candidates pay ₹1000/- (excluding convenience fee/bank commission and GST); SC/ST/PwBD candidates are not charged any fee.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for ONGC Geologist and Engineer posts?
faq.answer: Selection is through a Computer Based Test (CBT), followed by an Interview, and finally Medical Fitness. CBT carries 85 marks and the Interview carries 15 marks.

faq.question: Q7. When will the ONGC CBT exam be conducted?
faq.answer: The CBT is tentatively scheduled for 6 September 2026.

faq.question: Q8. Is there negative marking in the ONGC CBT?
faq.answer: No, there is no negative marking in the CBT.

faq.question: Q9. What is the qualification required for ONGC Geology posts?
faq.answer: A Post Graduate Degree in Geology, or M.Sc./M.Tech in Petroleum Geoscience, Petroleum Geology, or Geological Technology, each with a minimum of 60% marks.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply for ONGC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online only at www.ongcindia.com between 17 July and 31 July 2026. No offline or paper applications are accepted.
